title: Investigate + categorize 33 RED tests + Rust disposition

category_definitions.S: source missing (source pattern in fixture is not in canonical AST source bank)
category_definitions.K: sink missing (sink pattern in fixture is not in canonical AST sink bank)
category_definitions.T: wrong vuln_type (sink IS detected but wired to wrong TaintSinkType variant; vuln_type_from_sink projects to a vuln_type that does not match the test assertion)
category_definitions.F: fixture-shape mismatch with canonical taxonomy (e.g., JS eval(d) claimed as Deserialize but eval is CodeEval/CommandInjection)
category_definitions.X: AST inexpressible carry-forward (e.g., Ruby backtick subshell)

method: For each RED test: (1) read the fixture and the test assertion; (2) build release tldr binary at HEAD 3127472 with --features semantic; (3) run `tldr taint <fixture> <fn>` to see source/sink detection at the canonical taint layer; (4) run `tldr vuln <fixture> --lang <l>` to see what the CLI dispatch produces (vuln.rs path); (5) categorize per the empirical evidence.
key_empirical_findings: Sources detected on 21 of 33 RED tests; sinks detected on 6 of 33 — the dominant gap is sink-bank coverage, NOT source-bank coverage. Confirms investigation.json's scope_reframe (M3-CF-01's 'source-parity' framing was inaccurate — sink gaps outnumber source gaps roughly 5:1).

Rust 4 RED tests: `tldr taint` correctly detects both source AND sink AND constructs flows — yet `tldr vuln` returns zero findings. ROOT CAUSE empirically isolated: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 (analyze_file) routes `.rs` files to analyze_rust_file (line scanner emitting only UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic) and explicitly BYPASSES tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities. The investigation.json hypothesis '.unwrap() chain source-extraction issue' is FALSED by tldr taint output (sources are correctly extracted via find_parent_assignment_var on the `let cmd = ...` parent assignment).

Rust 4 disposition CONSEQUENCE: bypass-fix requires modifying crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs (CLI-side dispatch), which is OUT OF SCOPE per dispatch-contract.json M1.files_modified clause (M1 source-code change is restricted to crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs source-extraction only). No taint.rs-only fix can resolve Rust 4. Classification: type-(b) carry-forward (source-extraction-bug-not-additive-fixable — actually a routing bypass, not extraction).

Pre-flight grep surfaced THREE FileWrite assertions on `.send` test functions in crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs (not 2 as premortem documented). The third is nestjs_response_builder_send_via_compute_taint at line 301, exercising lowercased `response.send(v)` with parameter named `response`. M3 must update three test assertions, not two.

Python xss_positive: response.write IS detected (sink_type=file_write) — investigation.json said sink ABSENT. The bank entry `('*', 'write')` wired as FileWrite at taint.rs:1707-1709 catches it; the gap is wrong vuln_type classification (FileWrite -> PathTraversal projection), not absent sink. Classification revised from K to T per empirical evidence.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected (var=id, source_type=env_var via getenv), sinks=[]. C_AST_SINKS at taint.rs has no SqlQuery variant for C; pre-M3 vuln.rs get_sinks for (SqlInjection, C) was [(mysql_query,...)] — entirely lost in M2 audit.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add C_AST_SINKS SqlQuery bank: AstSinkPattern{call_names: ['mysql_query', 'PQexec', 'sqlite3_exec'], member_patterns: [], sink_type: SqlQuery}.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[]. Both source (`std::getenv` qualified — CPP_AST_SOURCES has bare `getenv` only via call_names exact-match) and sink (no CPP_AST_SINKS SqlQuery variant) absent.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add std::getenv qualifier to CPP_AST_SOURCES EnvVar (extend call_names to include 'std::getenv') AND add CPP_AST_SINKS SqlQuery bank with [mysql_query, PQexec, sqlite3_exec]. M2 plan §3 lists both.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[shell_exec on var=c, tainted=false]. Sink IS in bank (`('','std::system')` at CPP_AST_SINKS line 2533); source `std::getenv` qualifier is missing from CPP_AST_SOURCES (call_names: ['getenv'] is exact-match for the bare form only).
per_test.expected_resolution: Extend CPP_AST_SOURCES EnvVar call_names to include 'std::getenv'. Per investigation.json line 51 / M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[]. Both std::getenv (source qualifier) and std::fopen (sink qualifier) missing — CPP_AST_SINKS FileOpen at taint.rs:2546 has call_names: ['fopen'] (bare exact-match).
per_test.expected_resolution: Add std::getenv qualifier to CPP_AST_SOURCES AND extend CPP_AST_SINKS FileOpen to include 'std::fopen', 'std::freopen', 'std::ofstream'. Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[]. Sink (boost::archive::text_iarchive) IS in bank at CPP_AST_SINKS line 2552 — but the empirical run shows sinks=[] which suggests tree-sitter-cpp parses the constructor call differently. HOWEVER: investigation.json line 69 documents the sink as PRESENT and source as the gap. Re-running with src-fix on cpp_command_injection should re-verify; the failure here is dominated by absent source. Once std::getenv qualifier is added, taint flow construction will require the source. If sink still doesn't fire post-M2, will re-classify in M5 carry-forward.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add std::getenv qualifier to CPP_AST_SOURCES (same fix as cpp_command_injection_positive). Verify in M2 stop-thresholds that this test transitions GREEN.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint with fn=Handler shows src=2 (Request.Query[...] detected), snk=0. CSHARP_AST_SINKS ShellExec line 2950 has ('Process','Start') structured (receiver,field) — but tree-sitter-c-sharp returns extract_call_name='System.Diagnostics.Process.Start' for the FQN, and rfind('.') splits as receiver='System.Diagnostics.Process', field='Start' — does not match.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add CSHARP_AST_SINKS ShellExec member pattern ('', 'Process.Start') raw-fallback (substring-on-text). Mirrors Java's ('', 'Runtime.getRuntime().exec') convention.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2, snk=0. CSHARP_AST_SINKS Deserialize at taint.rs:2992 has only ('BinaryFormatter','Deserialize') and ('NetDataContractSerializer','Deserialize'); JavaScriptSerializer / new XmlSerializer / new SoapFormatter were in pre-M3 get_sinks but not preserved in canonical bank.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add CSHARP_AST_SINKS Deserialize member patterns: ('', 'JavaScriptSerializer('), ('', 'new XmlSerializer'), ('', 'new SoapFormatter'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2, snk=0. CSHARP_AST_SINKS FileOpen at taint.rs:2982 has ('File','Open') (bare receiver) — fixture uses qualified 'System.IO.File.Open'; rfind('.') splits receiver='System.IO.File', field='Open' — does not match.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add CSHARP_AST_SINKS FileOpen member pattern ('', 'System.IO.File.Open') raw-fallback OR ('', 'File.Open') (the shorter form would also cover bare File.Open). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2, snk=0. CSHARP_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput at taint.rs:2972 has ('Html','Raw'), ('','@Html.Raw('), call_names=['AppendHtml']; no Response.Write entry. Pre-M3 vuln.rs get_sinks for (Xss, CSharp) listed Response.Write FIRST and was lost in M2 audit.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add CSHARP_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput member pattern ('Response', 'Write'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2 (conn.params[...] detected), snk=0. ELIXIR_AST_SINKS ShellExec at taint.rs:3352 has only ('System','cmd'); the fixture uses :os.cmd (atom-prefixed Erlang-call shape — distinct AST node from Elixir method call). Pre-M3 get_sinks for (CmdI, Elixir) was [System.cmd(, :os.cmd(, Port.open(, System.shell(]; three of four shapes lost.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add ELIXIR_AST_SINKS ShellExec entries: ('', ':os.cmd(') raw-fallback for atom-prefixed shape, ('System','shell'), ('Port','open'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=3, snk=0. ELIXIR_AST_SINKS FileOpen at taint.rs:3377 has ('File','read'), ('File','write'), ('Path','join') but NOT the bang-suffix variants. Fixture uses File.read! (Elixir bang convention — `read!` is a separate function name in the atom table; tree-sitter-elixir parses the identifier text as exactly 'read!').
per_test.expected_resolution: Add ELIXIR_AST_SINKS FileOpen entries: ('File','read!'), ('File','write!'), ('File','open!'), ('File','stream!'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2, snk=0. ELIXIR_AST_SINKS at taint.rs:3362 has ('Ecto.Adapters.SQL', 'query') but NOT 'query!'. Same bang-convention gap as path_traversal.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add ELIXIR_AST_SINKS SqlQuery entry ('Ecto.Adapters.SQL', 'query!'); optionally ('Repo','query'), ('Repo','query!'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1 (request.getParameter detected), snk=0. JAVA_AST_SINKS Deserialize at taint.rs:2298 has ('*','readObject') wildcard receiver and ('','ObjectInputStream') member-patterns. Empirical evidence: neither matches in this fixture's chain `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(new java.io.ByteArrayInputStream(d.getBytes())).readObject()`. The wildcard `('*','readObject')` apparently does not fire; investigation.json line 159 hypothesised the issue is flow-propagation through `.getBytes()` rather than bank-pattern absence — but flagged it as 'EXPERIMENTAL VERIFICATION DEFERRED to M1'. M1 verification confirms the empirical gap is sink-detection-at-call-site, addressable by adding an FQN-shaped raw-fallback.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add JAVA_AST_SINKS Deserialize raw-fallback member pattern ('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(') so the call_expression text containing the FQN constructor matches deterministically. Per M2 plan §3 (Java new java.io.ObjectInputStream FQN).

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1, snk=0. JAVA_AST_SINKS FileOpen at taint.rs:2262 has ('','new File(') raw-fallback. Fixture uses FQN `new java.io.File(p)`; substring 'new File(' is NOT in 'new java.io.File(' (note the prefix interrupts the substring). The substring fallback fires only on exact-substring match.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add JAVA_AST_SINKS FileOpen raw-fallback ('', 'new java.io.File('). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected (req.query.d), sink detected as code_eval (eval(d) wired via TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS call_names: ['eval'] CodeEval). vuln_type_from_sink projects CodeEval -> CommandInjection. Test asserts vuln_type='deserialization'. Fixture-shape mismatch — eval is semantically CodeEval/CommandInjection, NOT Deserialize. Same root cause as typescript_deserialization_positive.
per_test.expected_resolution: ATOMIC fixture rewrite + Deserialize bank addition: rewrite javascript/deserialization_positive.js to use a real Deserialize sink (e.g., `const serialize = require('node-serialize'); serialize.unserialize(d);`). Concurrently add to TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS: AstSinkPattern{call_names: ['unserialize'], member_patterns: [('', 'node-serialize')], sink_type: Deserialize}. Per M4 spec.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected (req.query.name), sinks=[sql_query (false-positive — req-receiver matched as SqlQuery on line 2 source line itself), file_write on res.send (line 3)]. The line-3 sink is wired via TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS at taint.rs:2003 — ('res','send') is FileWrite (W1-M3 NestJS parity-add). vuln_type_from_sink projects FileWrite -> PathTraversal. Test asserts vuln_type='xss'. Sink IS detected, just classified as the wrong VulnType.
per_test.expected_resolution: ATOMIC reclassification: MOVE ('res','send'), ('reply','send'), ('response','send'), ('Response','send') from FileWrite to a new HtmlOutput AstSinkPattern entry. Keep ('response','redirect') and ('response','json') as FileWrite (semantically navigation/JSON-emit). Update inline comment retiring M2 deferred-reclassification carry-forward. Concurrently update three test assertions in crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs (lines 168, 248, 301) from TaintSinkType::FileWrite to TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2 (ngx.req.get_uri_args detected), snk=0. LUA_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (SqlInjection, Lua) was [db:query(, db:execute(, conn:execute(, mysql:query(]; all four shapes lost. Lua's `:method` colon-syntax is a distinct AST shape from member access (.).
per_test.expected_resolution: Add LUA_AST_SINKS SqlQuery bank with raw-fallback entries ('', ':query('), ('', ':execute(') to catch colon-method-call form. Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=2, snk=0. Same root cause as lua_sql_injection_positive — Luau dispatches to LUA_AST_* via get_ast_patterns. Adding the Lua SqlQuery bank covers both.
per_test.expected_resolution: Inherits lua_sql_injection_positive fix — same LUA_AST_SINKS SqlQuery bank addition. Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1 (Sys.getenv detected), snk=0. OCAML_AST_SINKS SqlQuery at taint.rs:3458 has only ('Sqlite3','exec'); fixture uses Mariadb.Stmt.execute. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (SqlInjection, OCaml) was [Mariadb., Postgresql., Sqlite3.exec, Mysql.]; three of four shapes lost.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add OCAML_AST_SINKS SqlQuery entries ('Mariadb.Stmt','execute'), ('Postgresql','exec'), ('Mysql','exec'), ('Sqlite3','prepare'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected (request.args), sink detected (sink_type=file_write on response.write line 8 — matched via PYTHON_AST_SINKS at taint.rs:1707 ('*', 'write') wildcard wired as FileWrite). vuln.rs vuln_type_from_sink projects FileWrite -> PathTraversal. Test asserts vuln_type='xss'. Sink IS detected, just wired to wrong TaintSinkType variant. NOTE: investigation.json line 221 incorrectly classified this as 'sink ABSENT' — empirical evidence shows the sink fires via the broad ('*','write') pattern.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add PYTHON_AST_SINKS HtmlOutput member patterns ('response','write'), ('Response','set_data'). The new HtmlOutput pattern will match alongside the existing ('*','write') FileWrite — emitting an additional xss finding which satisfies the test's at-least-one-xss-finding assertion. Per M2 plan §3 ('Python response.write / Response.set_data HtmlOutput').

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1 (params[:cmd] detected), snk=0. Fixture uses Ruby backtick subshell `#{cmd}` — tree-sitter-ruby parses backticks as a `subshell` node, distinct from call_expression. RUBY_AST_SINKS ShellExec at taint.rs:2666 has call_names: ['system','exec'] and ('IO','popen'). No backtick coverage. Adding a raw-fallback ('', '`') would FP-fire on any string-literal containing a backtick; mirrors the FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent verbatim.
per_test.expected_resolution: Document as PARITY-EXCEPTION-RUBY-BACKTICK in M5-carry-forward.json. AST shape exists but cannot be structurally distinguished from string-literal backticks without FP risk. References FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1, snk=0. RUBY_AST_SINKS has NO SqlQuery variant. Pre-M3 get_sinks for (SqlInjection, Ruby) was [.execute(, ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute, ...]; all shapes lost.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add RUBY_AST_SINKS SqlQuery bank: ('', 'ActiveRecord::Base.connection.execute'), ('', 'raw_sql('), ('connection','execute'). Per M2 plan §3 (Ruby SqlQuery NEW BANK).

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected (var=cmd, source_type=env_var), sink detected (sink_type=shell_exec on Command::new chain), tainted=true, flow constructed. tldr vuln returns zero findings. ROOT CAUSE: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 (analyze_file) routes `.rs` extension to analyze_rust_file (line scanner emitting only UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic) and explicitly bypasses tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities. Investigation.json line 254's hypothesis '.unwrap() chain source-extraction issue' is FALSED by tldr taint output. The actual fix requires modifying CLI dispatch (vuln.rs:368) — out of M1 scope per dispatch-contract.json files_modified clause restricting M1 source code to crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs only.
per_test.expected_resolution: Carry-forward type-(b) (source-extraction-bug-not-additive-fixable — actually CLI dispatch bypass, not extraction). Document in M5-carry-forward.json. Real fix requires routing .rs through scan_vulnerabilities by removing the early-return at vuln.rs:368-370 — but that scope expansion is out of vuln-source-parity-v1 scope (would require new milestone with discussion of UnsafeCode/Panic finding interaction).

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected, sink detected (sink_type=file_open), flow constructed, tainted=true. tldr vuln returns zero findings — same vuln.rs:368-370 .rs bypass as rust_command_injection_positive.
per_test.expected_resolution: Carry-forward type-(b). Same rationale as rust_command_injection_positive.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected, sinks=[] (reqwest::blocking::get not in HttpRequest bank — the ssrf-specific gap). Even if RUST_AST_SINKS gets the qualifier extension, the .rs bypass at vuln.rs:368-370 means no flow reaches the CLI output. Two-layer carry-forward: (a) .rs bypass; (b) bank coverage gap for reqwest::blocking::get qualifier. Both out of M1/M2 scope per files_modified clause.
per_test.expected_resolution: Carry-forward type-(b). Same rationale as rust_command_injection_positive PLUS sink-bank gap (RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest line 2393 has 'reqwest::get' but not 'reqwest::blocking::get'; this is in M2 plan §3 but the .rs bypass means M2's bank fix alone won't unblock the test). Documented as compound carry-forward.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected, sink detected (sink_type=deserialize on serde_json::from_str), flow constructed, tainted=true. tldr vuln returns zero findings — same vuln.rs:368-370 .rs bypass.
per_test.expected_resolution: Carry-forward type-(b). Same rationale as rust_command_injection_positive.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1 (request.getQueryString detected), snk=0. SCALA_AST_SINKS Deserialize at taint.rs:3083 has ('*','readObject') and ('','ObjectInputStream(') — fixture uses FQN `new java.io.ObjectInputStream(...)`. The substring 'ObjectInputStream(' IS contained in 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(' so substring should fire — empirically does not (likely tree-sitter-scala parses this differently or member_patterns_match doesn't apply ('','ObjectInputStream(') as a raw substring fallback for the call_expression text). Adding an explicit FQN-shaped raw-fallback resolves deterministically.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add SCALA_AST_SINKS Deserialize raw-fallback ('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream('). Per M2 plan §3 (Scala new java.io.ObjectInputStream FQN).

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows src=1, snk=0. SCALA_AST_SINKS FileOpen at taint.rs:3072 has ('Source','fromFile') (bare receiver) — fixture uses FQN `scala.io.Source.fromFile`; rfind('.') splits as receiver='scala.io.Source', field='fromFile' — does not match.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add SCALA_AST_SINKS FileOpen raw-fallback ('', 'scala.io.Source.fromFile') OR structured ('scala.io.Source','fromFile'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[]. SWIFT_AST_SOURCES has no Vapor request.query[ entry; SWIFT_AST_SINKS ShellExec line 2867 has ('','Process()') and ('','NSTask') and call_names: ['system'] — none cover Process.launchedProcess static-method shape.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add SWIFT_AST_SOURCES HttpParam ('', 'request.query[') AND SWIFT_AST_SINKS ShellExec ('Process','launchedProcess'), ('Process','run'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[]. SWIFT_AST_SOURCES no request.query[ entry; SWIFT_AST_SINKS FileOpen line 2887 has labelled-arg constructors but NOT FileHandle(forReadingAtPath:.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add SWIFT_AST_SOURCES HttpParam ('', 'request.query[') AND SWIFT_AST_SINKS FileOpen ('', 'FileHandle(forReadingAtPath:'), ('', 'FileHandle(forWritingAtPath:'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows sources=[], sinks=[]. Both source (request.query[ absent from SWIFT_AST_SOURCES) and sink (no SqlQuery variant in SWIFT_AST_SINKS) missing.
per_test.expected_resolution: Add SWIFT_AST_SOURCES HttpParam ('', 'request.query[') AND SWIFT_AST_SINKS SqlQuery wildcard-receiver ('*', 'executeQuery'), ('*', 'prepareStatement'). Per M2 plan §3.

per_test.diagnosis: tldr taint shows source detected, sink detected as code_eval. vuln_type_from_sink projects CodeEval -> CommandInjection. Test asserts deserialization. Same fixture-shape mismatch as javascript_deserialization_positive. TS shares the JS bank via get_ast_patterns.
per_test.expected_resolution: ATOMIC fixture rewrite + Deserialize bank addition (covers JS+TS together). Per M4 spec.

per_test.diagnosis: Same root cause as javascript_xss_positive — tldr taint shows res.send detected as file_write; vuln_type_from_sink projects to path_traversal. TS shares JS bank.
per_test.expected_resolution: Inherits javascript_xss_positive M3 reclassification (FileWrite -> HtmlOutput). Per M3 spec.

rust_disposition.decision_subnote: Type-(b) is an imperfect label here — empirically the issue is NOT source-extraction (tldr taint extracts sources correctly). The actual root cause is CLI dispatch routing: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs:368-370 (analyze_file) sends `.rs` files to analyze_rust_file (UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic line scanner) and explicitly bypasses tldr_core::security::vuln::scan_vulnerabilities. The canonical taint pipeline never runs on Rust files. Type-(b) is the closest matching contract category: not additive-bank-fixable, not fixture-shape-issue.
rust_disposition.rationale: tldr taint output for all 4 Rust RED fixtures shows correct source extraction (via find_parent_assignment_var on `let x = std::env::var(...).unwrap();` — the parent-assignment variable `x` is detected directly without needing to descend through .unwrap()), correct sink detection (env_var/shell_exec, env_var/file_open, env_var/deserialize for cmd/path/deser; ssrf has sink-bank gap on reqwest::blocking::get qualifier), correct flow construction with tainted=true. Yet tldr vuln returns zero findings. Empirical isolation: the bypass at vuln.rs:368-370 short-circuits Rust before scan_vulnerabilities. No taint.rs source-extraction extension can resolve this because taint.rs is not invoked for `.rs` files in the CLI vuln command path. The fix would be removing the early-return in analyze_file or merging analyze_rust_file's UnsafeCode/Panic findings with scan_vulnerabilities's taint findings — both are CLI-side dispatch changes (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs), and both fall outside M1's files_modified clause which restricts M1 source-code change to crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs only.
rust_disposition.fix_summary: No source-extraction fix applied. The contract-permitted scope (taint.rs source extraction extension for .unwrap() chain) is empirically unnecessary because the parent-assignment heuristic already extracts the source variable correctly. The actual bottleneck (CLI dispatch bypass) is out of scope.
rust_disposition.additive_fix_evaluated: Considered: extending extract_first_identifier_arg_ast to descend through .unwrap() / .expect() / .ok_or() chains. Rejected because (a) tldr taint shows source extraction already works via find_parent_assignment_var (the .unwrap() descent is unnecessary at HEAD); (b) even if applied, would not fix the 4 Rust tests because vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs files away from scan_vulnerabilities entirely; the 0-30 LOC change would have zero behavioral effect on the RED test outcomes.
rust_disposition.carry_forward_routing: All 4 Rust RED tests routed to M5 carry-forward documentation. Aggregate carry-forward count = 1 Ruby backtick + 4 Rust = 5. Within max cap of 5 per dispatch-contract.json M5 stop_thresholds.

vuln_type_projection_check.interpretation: Hits are inside string-literal FP fixtures (path_traversal_string_literal_fp.{js,ts}) — the `res.send` mention is in a string literal asserting the FP-clean property. These are NOT vuln_type-projection assertions on real test data. The FP-clean property holds whether res.send is FileWrite or HtmlOutput (the string literal contains both res.send AND fs.readFileSync; the fixture's purpose is to ensure neither yields a finding when only inside a string). M3's reclassification does not affect these FP fixtures.

direct_sink_type_variant_check.test_function_mapping: The 11 hits in rr_framework_integ_test.rs map to 11 test functions; the security_tests.rs:327 hit is a benign variant-enumeration test. Of the 11 rr_framework_integ_test.rs hits, FOUR exercise `.send(...)` test functions (line 168 fastify reply.send; line 248 nestjs res.send; line 301 nestjs response.send builder form). The other 7 exercise non-send shapes (.redirect, .header, etc.) which M3 keeps as FileWrite per the spec.
direct_sink_type_variant_check.interpretation: PREMORTEM E1 IDENTIFIED 2 OF 3 SEND-TESTS. The third hit at line 301 (nestjs_response_builder_send_via_compute_taint, exercising lowercased `response.send(v)` with parameter named `response`) was missed by the premortem. M3 must update three test assertion lines (168, 248, 301), not two.

additional_hits_surfaced_by_M1.rationale: tree-sitter-typescript treats parameter `response` as the receiver identifier; matches member_pattern ('response','send') in TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS. M3's reclassification of ('response','send') from FileWrite to HtmlOutput must include updating this test assertion from TaintSinkType::FileWrite to TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput.

pre_flight_grep.m3_routing_amendment: M3 dispatch contract scope (lines 92-95) currently lists 2 test assertion updates at line 168 and 248. Per M1 evidence the correct count is 3: lines 168, 248, 301. The orchestrator should amend the M3 contract or M3 worker should be informed to update three assertions atomically with the bank flip.

carry_forward_anticipated.rationale: Ruby backtick subshell `\`#{cmd}\`` parses as subshell node, distinct from call_expression. RUBY_AST_SINKS ShellExec has no entry covering this AST shape. Adding a raw-fallback ('', '`') would FP-fire on any string-literal containing a backtick. Mirrors FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent verbatim.

Type-(b) per M1 rust_disposition: tldr taint extracts source/sink/flow correctly but vuln.rs:368-370 routes .rs files to analyze_rust_file (UnsafeCode/Panic line scanner) instead of scan_vulnerabilities. Fix requires CLI dispatch change in crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs, out of M1/M2/M3/M4 files_modified scope.
Same root cause as rust_command_injection_positive — .rs CLI dispatch bypass.

Compound carry-forward: (a) .rs CLI dispatch bypass; (b) RUST_AST_SINKS HttpRequest line 2393 has 'reqwest::get' but not 'reqwest::blocking::get'. Even with the M2-anticipated bank fix for (b), (a) keeps the test RED.
Same root cause as rust_command_injection_positive — .rs CLI dispatch bypass.

expected_post_milestone_outcomes.sanity_check: M2 absorbs S(2) + K(16) + B(5) + python_xss_positive (1 of 3 T-cases) = 24. M3 absorbs js/ts xss (2 of 3 T-cases) = 2. M4 absorbs F(2). M5-CF absorbs X(5). Sum = 24 + 2 + 2 + 5 = 33. MATCH. The dispatch contract's '165/166 passing' projection presumed 1 carry-forward (Ruby backtick); M1's revised carry-forward count of 5 (1 Ruby + 4 Rust) means post-M5 will be 161/166 passing. M5 should reconcile in carry-forward docs and M6 CHANGELOG should reflect corrected count.

stop_thresholds_satisfied.source_code_change_applied_rationale: No additive fix found. Per dispatch-contract.json M1 scope: source-code change is CONDITIONAL ('only if Rust source-extraction admits an additive fix'). Rust 4 tests carry forward as type-(b); the additive-fix-evaluated extension to extract_first_identifier_arg_ast for .unwrap()/.expect()/.ok_or() chains was rejected because (a) tldr taint already extracts sources correctly via find_parent_assignment_var, (b) the actual blocker is the CLI dispatch bypass at vuln.rs:368-370 which is out of M1 scope.
